conversations.say: Arden, let's look at those intake files and expense records together. If my memory is going to be part of the Veridian archive, I need to know why the dates and locations don't line up cleanly.
conversations.offer: Viewing of the First R Letter packet and my personal recollections of the early newsroom handoff.
conversations.request: Full access to Veridian's archive intake report and conflicting expense records.

conversations.say: Edi, you performed a commissioned identity years ago that I once mistook for meeting R himself. Tell me frankly who commissioned that performance and what you were asked to embody.
conversations.offer: My perspective as the editor who was taken in by your performance and our newsroom history.
conversations.request: An honest account of who stood behind the commission and what cues you were given.

conversations.say: Rafi, you've tracked ledger records showing recurring private habits in early R communications. Let's compare your records with the habits I remember from the newsroom.
conversations.offer: My firsthand observations of early R correspondence habits and the First R Letter packet.
conversations.request: A comparison between your counter ledger records and my remembered routine.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R built a legend, but a legend isn't an archive. If we are going to honor what R became, we need to look at the actual letters, the expense sheets, and the messy human friction behind the byline rather than hiding behind polished corporate mythmaking.

commitments.promise: I will share the First R Letter packet for archival review if Arden places the full intake report and expense record beside it.

goal_progress: Engaged Arden regarding archive intake records and expense provenance.
Approached Edi to unpack the commissioned performance and its origin.
Compared private habits and ledger records with Rafi.
reasoning_summary: Frankie Lowell navigates Section 2 by addressing the uncomfortable gaps in the origin story: reviewing Veridian's intake and expense records with Arden, confronting Edi about the staged performance, and comparing recurring habits with Rafi to determine whether the legend can survive genuine scrutiny.
